result: I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining hate without first feeling the full weight of what it does to the other person—an involuntary, undeniable mirroring of their pain. Not a removal of free will, but an inescapable bracket of empathy: you can still choose cruelty, but only after you’ve tasted the blood in their mouth, felt the vertigo of their sleepless 3 a.m., worn the scar tissue of their worst day under your own skin. Overnight, revenge campaigns, pogroms, street-corner beatings, casual spite—all would stall at the gate because the perpetrator would double over with the mirrored wound before the fist could finish its arc. The world wouldn’t become perfect; people would still disagree, compete, even fight. But no one would get to kill, oppress, or humiliate while remaining emotionally innocent. One generation growing up under that single rule and history’s needle would tilt; children would learn that whatever pain they inflict returns to them in high fidelity, so they’d invent new arguments, not new cruelties. That’s the change I’d make: not a utopia handed down, but a forcing function that makes uttering “They deserve it” physically impossible unless you’re ready to endure exactly what “it” is.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
